{"decision":"drift-check: advisory (smoke alarm) vs blocking (fail-closed hook)","chosen":"**Advisory** (exit code only, never blocks; forbidden_implementations block)","rationale":"Single-user CLI lesson (architecture.md 2026-04-15): fail-closed hooks deny tool calls with no self-recovery; \"日常恢复成本 > 防偶尔跳步骤收益\". Drift is informational — surfaces desync, does not stop work. Wired i"}
outcome: **Advisory** (exit code only, never blocks; forbidden_implementations block)

context: {"decision":"ml-training: remove from registry vs index as source-only","chosen":"**Index it** (source-only, advisory-flagged)","rationale":"It HAS a CAPABILITY.md → scan-packs indexes it (14→16); step1_5b Tier 1 can load it. NOT a phantom. The source/skill asymmetry surfaces only as the (d) advisory WARN."}

{"decision":"P3 scope under AC conflict","chosen":"OPTION A: token-free blocks only (~10%)","rationale":"User chose A; avoids reframing SAFETY AC3.2; move only 0-token blocks so byte-identity trivially holds"}

{"decision":"constraint-bearing blocks (research_plan/express/experiment)","chosen":"leave INLINE","rationale":"they carry forbidden_implementations/VIOLATION; moving them needs the declined AC3.2 reframe"}
{"decision":"linter enforcement","chosen":"ADVISORY exit 0, never blocks","rationale":"single-user CLI mechanical-enforcement-rejected lesson; smoke alarm not suppressor"}
{"decision":"rule set","chosen":"A/B firm WARN (≥2× grounded) + C/D low-confidence INFO","rationale":"evidence-grounded only; avoid speculative-rule validation theater"}
